page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i4g.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance features 16 dedicated physical cores with 16 vCPUs, utilizing a 1-to-1 thread-to-core ratio. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and runs on a KVM hypervisor.

This instance includes 959 GB of bundled local SSD storage, offering low-latency data access directly on the host. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. The combination of dedicated CPU cores and local SSD storage provides predictable performance for I/O-sensitive operations without virtualization overhead from shared threads.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.i4g.4xlarge delivers a balanced allocation of compute and memory alongside integrated local storage. This configuration is suitable for workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution and local scratch space, such as relational databases, NoSQL data stores, medium-scale analytical processing, and general-purpose applications with moderate storage requirements.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i4g.4xlarge is an x86_64 server instance configured with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor operating at 2.7 GHz. Running on a KVM hypervisor, the instance provides 64.0 GB of system memory, equating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key feature of this instance is its 959 GB of bundled local SSD storage, which is paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. The dedicated CPU allocation and 1-to-1 thread-to-core ratio ensure stable, predictable processing performance. This hardware profile makes the instance highly suitable for storage-intensive and database workloads, such as transactional databases, local caching layers, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring low-latency local storage.
